description: The OpenSDK description: a language-agnostic intermediate representation (IR) of an API SDK — a nominal type system (named structs/enums/unions) plus a resource tree of typed methods carrying their HTTP request binding. Emitted by @xyd-js/openapi2opensdk and consumed by the opensdk-* code generators (Go first).

servers.description: Available API base URLs (from the OpenAPI servers list)

security.description: Default security requirements for the SDK

types.description: The symbol table: all named types (structs, enums, unions, aliases) referenced by resources/methods, resolved from OpenAPI component schemas and hoisted inline types

resources.description: The client resource tree (nested services, each with typed methods)

sdk.description: Declarative runtime behavior of the generated SDK (retry, timeout, errors, user-agent, ...). The converter always stamps defaults so emitters can rely on it; hand-written IR may omit it and use opensdk-core's sdkBehavior() accessor for defaults.

summary.description: A short summary of the API

homepage.description: The package/project homepage URL (publish metadata; no OpenAPI equivalent)

repository.description: The source repository URL (publish metadata; no OpenAPI equivalent)

NamedType.description: A named type in the symbol table. `kind` discriminates which fields apply.

name.description: The type name (source component key or a deterministic synthetic name for a hoisted inline type)

kind.description: The type kind a generator branches on: struct | enum | union | alias

semantics.description: kind=union: oneOf (exactly one) | anyOf (one or more)

discriminator.description: kind=union: the OpenAPI discriminator, when present

of.description: kind=alias: the aliased (newtype) target for a top-level scalar/array component

name.description: The field's wire name (a generator derives the language-specific field name)

nullable.description: Whether the field may be null

default.description: The field's default value, if any

TypeRef.description: A structural reference to a type. `kind` discriminates which fields apply.

format.description: kind=scalar: the OpenAPI format hint (e.g. date-time, binary, int64)

name.description: kind=ref: the referenced NamedType name (symbol-table lookup)

const.description: kind=scalar: a fixed literal value (JSON Schema const / single-value enum); emitters may auto-fill it

nullable.description: Whether the referenced value may be null

EnumValue.description: One value of a named enum.

name.description: An explicit constant name, if the wire value isn't a clean identifier

Discriminator.description: An OpenAPI discriminator for a union.

propertyName.description: The property whose value selects the variant

mapping.description: Map of discriminator value -> variant type name

Resource.description: A resource / service node in the client tree.

name.description: The resource name (a generator derives the service/field name)

Method.description: A typed SDK method plus the HTTP request binding needed to call the API.

action.description: The method verb: list | create | retrieve | update | delete | <custom>

operationId.description: The source OpenAPI operationId, when present

injectIdempotencyKey.description: The spec declared an idempotency-key header on this operation; the converter strips it from params and the runtime injects a generated key per sdk.idempotency

pathParams.description: Path parameters, in path order (positional method arguments)

queryParams.description: Query parameters (fields of the params struct)

primaryResponse.description: The success response type the method returns

pagination.description: Pagination shape, when the method is a pageable list

Param.description: A path/query/header parameter with its wire name (request binding) and type.

name.description: The identifier-safe parameter name (generators derive SDK identifiers from it)

wireName.description: The raw HTTP wire name when it differs from `name` (e.g. `ids[]`); emitters must send this on the wire

default.description: The parameter's default value, if any

explode.description: OpenAPI explode flag for array/object serialization

encoding.description: How the body is encoded: json | multipart | form

SdkBehavior.description: Declarative runtime behavior of the generated SDK. One source of truth for what every language's runtime does; emitters interpolate these values instead of hardcoding them.

RetryPolicy.description: Automatic retry behavior. Delay per attempt: min(initialDelayMs * multiplier^attempt, maxDelayMs), +/- jitter fraction; a Retry-After response header wins when honorRetryAfterHeader is true.

maxRetries.description: Maximum retry attempts after the first request

retryableStatusCodes.description: HTTP status codes that trigger a retry

honorRetryAfterHeader.description: Whether a Retry-After response header overrides the computed backoff

maxDelayMs.description: Upper bound on the computed delay

jitter.description: Random jitter fraction (0..1) applied to each delay

defaultTimeoutMs.description: Default per-request timeout; 0 means no deadline

timeoutEnvVar.description: Environment variable that overrides the default timeout (milliseconds)

ErrorPolicy.description: Maps HTTP statuses to named error kinds; every language raises/returns its idiomatic form of the same kind.

errorDocUrlTemplate.description: URL template for error docs (e.g. https://api.example.com/errors/{kind})

includeRuntimeVersion.description: Append the language runtime version (go1.22, python/3.12)

aiAgentEnvVars.description: Env var -> agent slug; when set at runtime the slug is appended to the UA for agent-traffic attribution

requestIdHeader.description: Response header carrying the request id, surfaced on errors

headerName.description: Request header carrying client telemetry (previous request id + latency)

enabledByDefault.description: Whether the client-telemetry loop is on by default

events.description: Closed lifecycle-event vocabulary every runtime must emit (request.start, request.success, request.retry, request.rate_limited, request.error, request.connection_error)

autoGenerateForPost.description: Auto-generate a key for retried POSTs on methods flagged injectIdempotencyKey

optionKeys.description: Request-option keys that must never appear inside request params (fail fast if seen)

Pagination.description: Pagination shape for a pageable list method.

style.description: The pagination style (e.g. cursor, page, offset)

itemsField.description: The response field holding the page items (e.g. data)

itemType.description: The paged element type (the itemsField array's element), resolved through the symbol table so emitters can produce Page[T]/auto-pagers

cursorParam.description: The request parameter carrying the cursor (e.g. after)

offsetParam.description: style=offset: the request parameter carrying the item offset (e.g. offset, skip)

limitParam.description: The request parameter carrying the page size (e.g. limit, page_size, per_page)

nextField.description: The response field indicating more pages (e.g. has_more, next)

SdkSecurity.description: A single security scheme mapped from the OpenAPI securitySchemes.

schemeName.description: The source securityScheme key (e.g. AdminApiKeyAuth) — distinguishes per-operation overrides whose scheme shape equals the default

kind.description: Normalized scheme a generator branches on: bearer | apiKey-header | apiKey-query | apiKey-cookie | basic | other

type.description: The OpenAPI security scheme type (e.g. http, apiKey, oauth2)

scheme.description: The HTTP auth scheme (e.g. bearer, basic) when type is http

in.description: Location for an apiKey scheme (header, query or cookie)

name.description: Header/query/cookie name for an apiKey scheme

bearerFormat.description: The bearer token format hint (e.g. JWT)

envVar.description: Environment variable a generated SDK reads the credential from (e.g. OPENAI_API_KEY)

name.description: The identifying name of the contact person/organization

url.description: The URI for the contact information

email.description: The email address of the contact person/organization
